title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Marina Gregg informs Detective Craddock that she believes she was the intended victim of a poisoning attempt, noting that while her drink was tampered with, a different woman ultimately consumed it. She explains that she felt compelled to come forward following advice from Jason Rad, Gilchrist, and Ginx. To support her claim, Marina presents a note found on her dressing table after a bath that reads, "Don't think you can escape next time." She suggests the note may have been left by someone in the house or an intruder who climbed onto her balcony.

Marina further discloses a history of threatening correspondence. Three weeks prior, a handwritten note saying "Get ready to die" was sent to her movie company. Additionally, on the day of a cocktail party, a man on a bicycle wearing glasses delivered a note stating, "Today is your last day alive." Marina claims she had previously dismissed these incidents as jokes or religious harassment.

Detectives Craddock and Morton express skepticism regarding Marina's claims of ignorance, suggesting her celebrity status provides numerous motives for enemies. Jason Rad joins the discussion to defend Marina, asserting they have no enemies and characterizing the situation as a joke that has turned dangerous. Finally, Detective Clutter questions Marina regarding a witness report that she appeared frightened while greeting the Baskervilles. The witness noted she was looking toward the stairs, where several individuals—including Mr. and Mrs. Glass, a reporter, Adele Fenn, and Laura Bruster—had recently appeared. Marina denies being frightened.
